name: 19_Time_Series_Forecasting_Seq2Seq_LSTM_Rossmann_ML
query: Develop a sales forecasting system using a sequence-to-sequence model based on LSTM with the Rossmann Store Sales dataset, downloading it from Kaggle [here](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) and loading it in `src/data_loader.py`. Split the data into training and testing sets and save them in `src/data_loader.py`. Apply a sequence-to-sequence model based on `LSTM` and save the trained model under the `models/saved_models/` directory. Save the forecast results as `results/figures/forecast_results.png`. Save a comparison plot between the predicted and actual values to `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`. Generate an HTML report that includes the prediction results and comparison plots, with some interactive elements for exploring different forecast horizons, and save it as `results/report.html`. Ensure the model is tuned to capture seasonal trends in the sales data.

requirements.criteria: The "Rossmann Store Sales" dataset is used, potentially downloaded from Kaggle [this link](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) and loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.
The data is split into training and testing sets and implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
A sequence-to-sequence model based on "LSTM" is used. Please save the trained model under the `models/saved_models/` directory.
The forecast results are plotted and saved as `results/figures/forecast_results.png`.
A comparison plot of predicted vs. actual values is saved as `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`.
An HTML report containing forecast results and comparison plots is generated and saved as `results/report.html`.
The HTML report should include interactive elements that allow users to explore different forecast horizons.

preferences.criteria: The model should be tuned to capture seasonal trends in the sales data for more accurate forecasting.

judge_stats.criteria: The "Rossmann Store Sales" dataset is used, potentially downloaded from Kaggle [this link](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) and loaded in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually implement loading of the Rossmann Store Sales dataset. It is a "minimal semantic implementation" placeholder that only defines requirement criteria strings and stub functions (`load_dataset()` returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any actual data loading logic). There is no code to download from Kaggle, read CSV files (e.g., `train.csv`, `store.csv`), or process the Rossmann dataset in any meaningful way. The file merely references the dataset name as a string constant rather than implementing actual data loading functionality.

reason:

judge_stats.criteria: The HTML report should include interactive elements that allow users to explore different forecast horizons.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project contains a `results/report.html` file, but there is no evidence that it includes interactive elements for exploring different forecast horizons. The only source file present (`src/data_loader.py`) is a minimal placeholder implementation that contains no HTML generation code, no JavaScript/interactive widget logic, and no references to libraries like Plotly, Bokeh, or D3.js that would enable interactive forecast horizon exploration. The trajectory analysis also confirms no relevant execution steps were recorded for interactive HTML report generation. The placeholder nature of the code (with stub functions like `load_dataset()` and `build_transforms()`) strongly indicates the report.html is not a functionally implemented interactive report.
